heading: Definitions
label: 2341 Definitions
notes: References in TextChapter 52 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, referred to in pars. (2)(A) and (7)(A), is classified generally to chapter 52 (§ 5701 et seq.) of Title 26, Internal Revenue Code.

Amendments2006—Par. (2). , which directed amendment of par. (2) by substituting “State or local cigarette taxes in the State or locality where such cigarettes are found, if the State or local government” for “State cigarette taxes in the State where such cigarettes are found, if the State” in introductory provisions, was executed by making the substitution for “State cigarette taxes in the State where such cigarettes are found, if such State”, to reflect the probable intent of Congress.Pub. L. 109–177, § 121(b)(6), substituted “10,000 cigarettes” for “60,000 cigarettes” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 109–177, § 121(a)(1)Pars. (6), (7). , added pars. (6) and (7).Pub. (2)(A). substituted “Internal Revenue Code of 1986” for “Internal Revenue Code of 1954”.Pub. L. 99–5141983—Par. (3). substituted “subtitle IV of title 49” for “the Interstate Commerce Act”.Pub. L. 97–449

children.label: A
children.text: any roll of tobacco wrapped in paper or in any substance not containing tobacco; and

children.label: B
children.text: any roll of tobacco wrapped in any substance containing tobacco which, because of its appearance, the type of tobacco used in the filler, or its packaging and labeling, is likely to be offered to, or purchased by, consumers as a cigarette described in subparagraph (A);

children.label: A
children.text: a person holding a permit issued pursuant to chapter 52 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 as a manufacturer of tobacco products or as an export warehouse proprietor, or a person operating a customs bonded warehouse pursuant to section 311 or 555 of the Tariff Act of 1930 ( or 1555) or an agent of such person;19 U.S.C. 1311

children.label: B
children.text: a common or contract carrier transporting the cigarettes involved under a proper bill of lading or freight bill which states the quantity, source, and destination of such cigarettes;

children.label: i
children.text: who is licensed or otherwise authorized by the State where the cigarettes are found to account for and pay cigarette taxes imposed by such State; and

children.label: ii
children.text: who has complied with the accounting and payment requirements relating to such license or authorization with respect to the cigarettes involved; or

children.label: D
children.text: an officer, employee, or other agent of the United States or a State, or any department, agency, or instrumentality of the United States or a State (including any political subdivision of a State) having possession of such cigarettes in connection with the performance of official duties;

the term “common or contract carrier” means a carrier holding a certificate of convenience and necessity, a permit for contract carrier by motor vehicle, or other valid operating authority under subtitle IV of title 49, or under equivalent operating authority from a regulatory agency of the United States or of any State;
the term “State” means a State of the United States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, or the Virgin Islands;
the term “Attorney General” means the Attorney General of the United States;
the term “smokeless tobacco” means any finely cut, ground, powdered, or leaf tobacco that is intended to be placed in the oral or nasal cavity or otherwise consumed without being combusted;

children.label: A
children.text: a person holding a permit issued pursuant to chapter 52 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 as manufacturer of tobacco products or as an export warehouse proprietor, a person operating a customs bonded warehouse pursuant to section 311 or 555 of the Tariff Act of 1930 (, 1555), or an agent of such person;119 U.S.C. 1311So in original. Probably should be “a manufacturer”.1

children.label: B
children.text: a common carrier transporting such smokeless tobacco under a proper bill of lading or freight bill which states the quantity, source, and designation of such smokeless tobacco;

children.label: i
children.text: is licensed or otherwise authorized by the State where such smokeless tobacco is found to engage in the business of selling or distributing tobacco products; and

children.label: ii
children.text: has complied with the accounting, tax, and payment requirements relating to such license or authorization with respect to such smokeless tobacco; or

children.label: D
children.text: an officer, employee, or agent of the United States or a State, or any department, agency, or instrumentality of the United States or a State (including any political subdivision of a State), having possession of such smokeless tobacco in connection with the performance of official duties;2So in original. The semicolon probably should be a period.2
